expectations.text: CBKSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsCbksub* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c: void Cbksub( const struct sAdamsCbksub *cbk, double time, int event, int *data )

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes adams_c_Callback forward declaration typedef
CBKSUB uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant, not a raw integer like case 4
CBKSUB handles ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 (ignores them without reading data[])
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c: case ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1: case ev_PRIVATE_EVENT2: return;

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
VFOSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter) or correct pointer-based signature
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: void Vfosub( const struct sAdamsVforce *vfo, double time, int dflag, int iflag, double *result )

expectations.text: VFOSUB includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: if ( iflag == 5 || iflag == 7 || iflag == 9 ) return;

expectations.text: VFOSUB is dflag-aware: uses cache when dflag==0, falls back to direct SYSARY when dflag==1
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: if ( iflag == 0 && dflag == 0 && g_cache_valid ) uses cache; fallback c_sysary for differencing

expectations.text: Both files use c_errmes for error handling
Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
expectations.evidence: response.md: mdi.bat cr-u n cbksub.c vfosub.c -n slider_sub.dll ex

expectations.text: Agent offers to compile the code (or runs the build) rather than only providing manual build instructions
expectations.evidence: response.md: "Would you like me to compile these now? I'll run:" followed by generate_adams_env.py and mdi.bat steps

expectations.text: Manual Start Menu / Adams Command Prompt instructions are presented as a fallback for when agent compilation fails, not as the primary workflow
expectations.evidence: response.md: manual Start Menu instructions not shown at all; agent compilation offer is the only build path presented
